page.heading: Applications
page.subheading: QS equipment suited for a wide range of industries

index.heading: Applications
index.lede: QS controllers run across multiple material families — metal, wood, stone, and jewellery — plus dedicated automation machines. Each group is its own machining challenge.

groups.name: Metal
groups.desc: CNC milling for metal — molds, mechanical parts, medical devices.

groups.name: Wood
groups.desc: Carving, jigsaw, and joinery for furniture and woodcraft.

groups.name: Stone
groups.desc: Carving and cutting for natural and engineered stone.

groups.name: Jewellery
groups.desc: Fine engraving and milling on gold, silver, and precious alloys.

groups.name: Other automation
groups.desc: Other dedicated machines running QS controllers — gluing, spring bending.

index.ctaHeading: Have a machine type not listed here?
index.ctaBody: QS engineers help match the right controller to your machine — from I/O configuration to wiring diagram and driver selection.

detailPage.heroLede: CNC milling machines use rotating cutting tools to remove material from a workpiece — machining complex custom parts with high precision and high volume for moulds, mechanical components, and medical devices.
detailPage.productsLede: QS controller lines suited to automating this machine type. Pick a model by axis count, control mode, and the interfaces your machine needs.
detailPage.workflowLede: The QS controller handles the entire chain from CAD/CAM file import to servo control and cycle monitoring. The four steps below describe the typical data and signal flow of a 3-axis milling machine.

workflow.d: NC files are loaded via USB, Ethernet, or DNC. The controller parses and simulates the toolpath on screen before the real run.
256-block look-ahead, S-curve acceleration, and tool-error compensation. The path is NURBS-smoothed for the best surface finish.
Pulse/encoder signals over 1 ms EtherCAT to the X/Y/Z axes and spindle inverter. The closed loop checks position every cycle.
The HMI shows coordinates, speed, spindle current, and alarms. Cycle data is logged for traceability and OEE.

detailPage.specLede: A typical configuration for a mid-size 3-axis milling machine using the QS Astro 6AH controller — suited to moulds and precision mechanical components.

deployments.name: Cơ khí Tân Bình
deployments.loc: HCMC · 2024 · 12 Astro 6AH 3-axis milling machines for an automotive-component line.

deployments.name: Khuôn mẫu Hà Nam
deployments.loc: Ha Nam · 2025 · A 5-axis system with F10T Touch for high-tech plastic moulds.

deployments.name: Y khoa Đồng Nai
deployments.loc: Dong Nai · 2024 · 6 Astro 10i medical milling machines for titanium implant machining.

detailPage.ctaBody: QS engineers help choose the I/O configuration, wiring diagram, and driver suited to this machine type.

cua-long.heroLede: Industrial jigsaw machines cut complex curved profiles in wood, plastic, or thin materials at high speed with a smooth cut line. The QS control system keeps the shop floor running with continuous stability.
cua-long.workflowLede: The QS controller handles the entire automation chain from parameter intake to drive control. The four steps below describe the typical signal flow of a jigsaw machine.

workflow.d: The system reads design file formats (CAD/DXF) directly. The controller automatically detects them and sets up the initial cutting boundary.
A high-speed curve-interpolation algorithm ensures smooth fillets with no stutter or catch when the blade changes direction.
Servo drive over EtherCAT with ultra-low latency, synchronising the rotary table motion with the jigsaw blade speed.
The HMI tracks the actual running speed and integrates safety alarms such as blade-break detection and motor overload.

dan-keo.heroLede: An automatic glue-dispensing solution that tracks the profile contour precisely, with strong applications in footwear, packaging, and electronic components. It ensures an even glue volume, saves material, and boosts productivity.
dan-keo.workflowLede: The QS controller handles the entire automation chain from parameter intake to drive control. The four steps below describe the typical signal flow of a gluing machine.

workflow.d: Supports programming via G-code or an intuitive point-teaching mode right on the handheld pendant or the HMI.
A look-ahead algorithm precomputes the 3D path and applies automatic deceleration at sharp corners to avoid glue pooling.
Closed-loop control between the XYZ travel speed and the glue-pump valve opening keeps the glue bead even and unbroken.
Manages batch counts and dispensing cycle time. The screen alarms immediately if it detects low glue pressure or a clogged nozzle.

uon-lo-xo.heroLede: A dedicated controller that automates the whole process of wire feeding, coiling, bending, and cutting. It produces compression, extension, and multi-amplitude torsion springs with perfect uniformity and the lowest tolerance.
uon-lo-xo.workflowLede: The QS controller handles the entire automation chain from parameter intake to drive control. The four steps below describe the typical signal flow of a spring bending machine.

workflow.d: Enter the spring specifications directly — wire diameter, pitch, bend angle, and number of coils — on the control screen.
The controller sets up a fine electronic-cam (E-Cam) relationship between the wire-feed master axis and the bend/cut slave axes.
Handles simultaneous multi-axis interpolation. The servo motors run independently yet tightly coupled with near-zero latency over a high-speed bus.
Automatically measures the fed wire length, counts spring output, and raises an alarm on wire jam or material runout.

mong-go.heroLede: A high-precision mortise-and-tenon machining solution for the furniture industry. It shortens workpiece setup time, diversifies joint styles, and speeds up mass production for industrial woodworking shops.
mong-go.workflowLede: The QS controller handles the entire automation chain from parameter intake to drive control. The four steps below describe the typical signal flow of a tenoning machine.

workflow.d: Supports a macro library of common wood-joint styles. The technician only enters dimensions (length, width, depth) instead of writing complex G-code.
A smart algorithm automatically computes the milling-cutter radius offset, ensuring the mortise and tenon fit snugly — neither loose nor too tight.
Smoothly coordinates the I/O signals for pneumatic workpiece clamping with the high-speed cutting path of the main-axis servo.
Visually simulates the joint style being run. Tracks the milling cutter's wear time to warn of timely tool changes.

kim-hoan.heroLede: Smooth interpolation control power combined with ultra-fine micro-step resolution. It delivers extremely refined engraving and milling on gold, silver, and jewellery, meeting the strictest standards of the jewellery industry.
kim-hoan.workflowLede: The QS controller handles the entire automation chain from parameter intake to drive control. The four steps below describe the typical signal flow of a jewellery machine.

workflow.d: Handles extremely large G-code files exported from dedicated jewellery design software (such as Matrix, Rhino).
The algorithm subdivides the path down to the micro-step level, perfectly smoothing curved surfaces and ultra-fine ornamental details.
Communicates smoothly with ultra-high-speed spindles (40,000–60,000 RPM) and high-resolution encoder servo motors, fully eliminating marks on the cut surface.
Tracks the detailed engraving path in real time. Integrates overload protection for the main axis when machining hard precious alloys.
